title: Unit 30 Learn Greek Grammar Masterclass: Compound Words & Prefixation

script.text: Welcome... to the Learn Greek Grammar Masterclass for Unit 30: Compound Words. Modern Greek is like a Lego set. It loves taking two or three words and snapping them together to create something entirely new. I'm William, and I'm joined by Athina. Athina... is it true that a Greek word can be literally as long as a sentence?
Historically, yes! Ancient Greek had some famous long words. Today, we keep it a bit more practical, but compounding is still our favorite way to create new meanings. We call them
Let's look at the mechanics. When we combine two words, we often use a 'connecting vowel'. Usually an 'o'. Athina, show us with 'Sea' and 'Wolf'.
We drop the feminine ending of sea, add an 'o', and we get:
An old sea dog! Notice the stress moved to the connecting vowel.
That’s a key point. What about describing people? Like a 'Red-head'?
It’s very descriptive. There are also many common prefixes that we use every day.
the apartment blocks you see everywhere in Athens. Literally, 'many-living' places.
means around. Like in the most famous Greek food!
It means 'turn' or 'circle'. When the meat turns on the spit, it’s doing a

Pro Tip: If you see a long word that looks scary, try to 'snap' it into two pieces. Usually, the first half is a description and the second half is the core noun. This concludes Unit 30. You now have the blueprints to build your own Greek vocabulary. Excellent work.
Μπράβο σας! Συνεχίζουμε δυνατά. Γεια σας!
